Submit Update
Home/Network

Find the Network

Use the directories that already save time.

Search high-value public shortcuts first, then use the 1493-entry local inventory as a working list to confirm before outreach.

Persona routes

Start from the person doing the work.

A new business, a gallery, a nonprofit, and a mentor program do not need the same first contact. Use these role shortcuts first, then narrow the local list by county, resource type, or access mode.

New businessExisting businessNonprofit or programArtist, gallery, or makerEvent organizerRural service or mentor
Download CSV Download JSON Travel listings Excel Travel listings CSV YellowPages outreach CSV YellowPages outreach Excel Sweep matrix Download Sources

Keep it current

If something has changed, send the update path.

The guide keeps practical links and contact routes visible so users have a starting point. Before spending money, printing materials, or promising placement, open the page and confirm current rules.

If a link is outdated, a business moved, or a better submission path exists, use the correction form and include the public source to check.

Submit a correction

Resource list

Search the local inventory, but verify before using.

Search by town, county, resource type, or access mode. Open the page when available and submit corrections when details have changed.

Need a table view? Open the public-contact appendix for county/community grouping.

Open the appendix

Listing updates

Submit or correct a listing

Use this when a business, nonprofit, gallery, program, service, venue, event series, or resource should be added, updated, corrected, or removed. Submissions are starting points for review, not automatic publication.

What to include

Name, county, community, website or source link, contact route, short description, audience served, and what action a reader should take.

Placeholder intake contact

Email: updates@statelineguide.example
Phone: (575) 000-0000
Mail: 000 Main Street, Raton, NM 87740

Open submission form

Next action

Choose the next practical route.

  • Find regional newsletters, calendars, visitor guides, and directory channels
  • Use copy-ready outreach templates
  • Submit a correction with a public source link
  • Open the public contact appendix

Search recipes

Search by the job, not only by the organization name.

Most users will not know the right agency, chamber, venue, or listing owner at first. These searches translate common goals into useful directory terms.

GoalSearch terms to tryWhat to do after finding a lead
Open or update a businessbusiness, license, city, chamber, economic developmentConfirm official requirements before promoting.
Promote a storefronttourism, visitor, directory, chamber, shop localCheck whether the channel reaches customers or visitors.
Post an eventcalendar, event, submit, venue, newsletterPrepare one packet before submitting.
Find funding or technical helpgrant, SBDC, SCORE, MainStreet, economic developmentVerify eligibility and deadline before promising a path.
Reach visitorstourism, visitor guide, lodging, attractions, chamberUse visitor-facing copy, photos, and travel-friendly details.
Find local partnersnonprofit, school, library, civic, arts, communityLead with mutual benefit and a specific next step.
Find artists or performersartist, gallery, music, venue, open mic, makerUse readiness tags and ask for the preferred contact route.

After a search result

A listing is a starting point, not proof that placement is available.

Verify the source

Open the public source link when available. Confirm that the page is current, the contact route still works, and the organization still serves the stated county or audience.

Match the route

Do not contact every listing. Choose the route that matches the job: calendar, directory, partner, media, tourism, funding, public office, or creative collaboration.

Save useful proof

Save the published link, screenshot, confirmation email, contact name, date submitted, and any response. These records make future outreach and reporting easier.

Back to top

Immediate directory

Find the right route.

Search by need, county, town, audience, or task. Results include practical links and update reminders so users can act without treating old details as final.

Results update after submitting the form or after a short pause while typing. Press Escape to close this panel.

Open resource directory Submit a correction

Colfax NM + Las Animas CO + Huerfano CO

This guide helps businesses, artists, nonprofits, programs, venues, and service providers find the right regional route faster.

See something outdated? Send a source link through Submit Update so the guide can improve.
